creator: raimeetravel
caption:
I don’t share this side of things very often, but I often get asked how to build a creator or content business so here’s my advice! #amdpartner 

I’ve worked so hard to build this while working other full time jobs, and how I started is very different than what I would do now to succeed! My @amd laptop has helped me so much along the way, and I just upgraded to this new one! 

I’ll always be a travel tips page, but if you ever have any creator questions, I’d love to help out in any way 🫶🏻 

#creatortips #creatorpreneur #contentcreatortips #contentstrategy

transcription.text: been making a full-time income from content for the past three years and each year that amount has doubled or tripled. So here's what I would do in 2026 if I wanted to replicate those results. I'm going to show you how with my AMD AI powered laptop that I use for everything. The very first thing I would do is get comfortable talking on camera. I was not good at this at first, but I do think if you want to succeed in 2026 onward, you need to be comfortable doing this. Number two, I would pick the two to three topics that you genuinely cannot stop thinking about or talking about and make sure that you talk about them from the standpoint of learn from me, not just look at me. And the third thing, you don't need to be perfect, but I would focus on having the right tools from the start. I use this laptop to help me do pretty much everything, editing video, designing graphics, using all of the AI features to help me plan my content. And it's super lightweight and portable so I can bring it anywhere all over the world with me. The battery also lasts. If you want the same one as me, comment AMD and I'll send you a link. I truly believe anyone can succeed at making a content business. Let me know if you have any other questions. I don't post about this kind of stuff often, but I figured it could be helpful.
